relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Passover approaches while leaders plot seizure by deceit.
They fear an uproar during the feast.
In Bethany, a woman breaks costly nard over Jesus’ head.
Some call devotion waste.
They scold her in the name of usefulness.
Jesus defends her beautiful work.
The poor remain as ongoing responsibility, but this moment is singular.
She prepares his body beforehand for burial.
Her memory travels with the good message.
Judas goes to hand him over.
Silver and opportunity join betrayal.
The disciples ask where to prepare Passover.
A water-bearer becomes the sign to follow.
The Teacher seeks the guest room.
The upper room is ready.
They find it as he said and prepare.
Evening gathers Jesus with the twelve.
Betrayal is named at the table.
Grief searches each heart: surely not I?
The betrayer shares the bowl.
The Son of Humanity goes as written, yet betrayal bears woe.
Bread is blessed, broken, given, and named as body.
The cup is shared with thanks.
The covenant blood is poured out for many.
New wine waits in the Divine reign.
They sing and go to the Mount of Olives.
The shepherd will be struck and the sheep scattered.
After rising, he will go before them to Galilee.
Peter insists he will not stumble.
Jesus names the coming threefold denial before the second rooster-sound.
Peter and the others promise beyond their strength.
Gethsemane becomes the place of prayer.
Jesus enters distress with Peter, James, and John nearby.
His soul is grieved to death, and he asks them to keep watch.
He falls to the ground and prays about the hour.
Abba receives the cup, the possibility, and the surrendered will.
He finds Peter sleeping instead of watching.
Watch and pray: spirit willing, flesh weak.
The prayer repeats.
Heavy eyes cannot answer.
The hour arrives; the Son of Humanity is handed over.
He rises toward the betrayer.
Judas arrives immediately with armed crowd.
A kiss is chosen as the sign.
Rabbi and kiss meet betrayal.
They seize Jesus.
A sword is drawn and the servant is struck.
Jesus exposes the arrest as though he were a robber.
Public temple teaching gives way to Scripture fulfillment.
All flee.
A young man follows wrapped in linen.
He leaves the linen and flees naked.
Jesus is led to the gathered authorities.
Peter follows from far away and warms himself by the fire.
Testimony is sought for death.
False witnesses do not agree.
More false witness rises.
The temple saying becomes accusation.
Even this testimony does not agree.
The high priest presses Jesus to answer.
Jesus remains silent until Messiah and Son identity are named.
I am, he says, and points to the Son of Humanity in clouds and power.
The high priest tears his garments.
They condemn him as liable to death.
Mockery and violence fall on him.
Peter remains below in the courtyard.
He is recognized as one with Jesus the Nazarene.
He denies knowing or understanding.
The servant girl names him again.
His Galilean identity gives him away.
The denial hardens into oath and curse.
The rooster sounds again, memory breaks open, and Peter weeps.
